Entrepreneurship Course Structure 2025/26
5-module curriculum · Both campuses
🗺
Module 1: Discover
Oct–Nov 2025 · Ideation & problem identification
🎯
Module 2: Position
Jan 22–Feb 12 · Market map, value prop, story
💪
Module 3: Commit
Feb–Mar · Resilience, MVP intro, commitment contracts
🔬
Module 4: Refine
Mar 26–Apr 23 · MVP, prototyping, financial literacy
🚀
Module 5: Launch
May 7–21 · Pitch mastery & launch readiness
🗓 Mock Pitch
Week of June 21, 2026 · Rabat (hybrid)
🏆 Final Pitch + Prize
September 2026 · Seed funding award
🎤 Masterclass
April 11, 2026 · Lilian U. Olubi

Wellbeing, Health & Inclusion

Holistic Support System

A comprehensive support ecosystem ensuring every scholar thrives — academically, personally, and professionally.

Mental & Physical Wellbeing
🧠
Mental Health Support
Individual and collective psychological support tailored to international scholars, refugees, and students navigating new cultural environments.
  • Regular well-being check-in sessions
  • Pre- and post-exam psychological support
  • Individual counselling on demand
  • Solid Minds partnership — mental health professionals
  • Crisis referral protocol in place
4
Sessions
12h
Total
0
Crises Feb–Mar
🏥
Medical Coverage
Full medical care included in the scholarship package — ensuring no scholar faces health barriers to academic success.
  • Full health insurance coverage
  • On-campus medical access (UM6P facilities)
  • Referral to specialists when needed
  • Disability-specific medical accommodations
  • Emergency health support protocol
40
Scholars Covered
100%
Coverage
🌸
Social Integration & Sisterhood
Building a strong community of belonging through cultural integration, peer support, and collective identity.
  • Darija language integration (23 sessions, 35h)
  • Lean-In Circles — Sisterhood groups
  • Community bootcamp (3 sessions, 18h)
  • Cultural activities & campus life
  • Peer mentoring network
23
Darija Sessions
35h
Integration
🛡
Safeguarding Framework
A comprehensive safeguarding policy ensuring a safe, respectful environment for all scholars, especially those from vulnerable backgrounds.
  • KCS (Keeping Children Safe) partnership
  • Policy reviewed & institutional adoption in progress
  • Benchmark with Makerere University
  • SAFE 360 focal point recruitment planned
  • Reporting mechanism being formalized
1
KCS Visit
In
Validation
Inclusion Targets & Achievements

Targets vs. Reality

The program has ambitious inclusion targets over 10 years — Year 1 laid the foundation while identifying operational gaps.

🌍 Geographic Diversity
5 African Regions
20%
Target per region
→
44%
North Africa Y1
Geographic rebalancing required. East Africa (Rwanda) well represented. SSA outreach missions in progress for Y2.
🏕 Refugees & Displaced
RDP Scholars
20%
10-yr target (≈115)
→
~5%
Est. Y1 enrollment
UNHCR DAFI partnership established. Refugee-Led Research Hub collaboration active. Protocol formalization in progress. South Sudan, Burkina Faso profiles enrolled.
♿ Disability Inclusion
Scholars with Disabilities
10%
10-yr target (≈57)
→
~2%
Est. Y1 (1–2 scholars)
Benchmark with UPHF completed. Accessibility diagnostic in progress. Light for the World partnership in development. Specific accommodations being formalized.
🇲🇦 Moroccan Scholars
Local Representation
15%
10-yr target (≈86)
→
32.5%
Y1 (13 scholars)
Target exceeded in Y1. 13 Moroccan scholars enrolled. May reduce proportionally as international pipeline scales in Y2–Y3.
Refugee & Displaced (RDP) — Action Plan
20% target · 10-year horizon
  • ✅
    UNHCR DAFI partnership — tracking and supporting refugee scholars through established protocol
  • ✅
    Refugee-Led Research Hub — best practices exchange with other universities
  • ✅
    Individualized profiles — vulnerable scholars tracked with personalized support plans
  • 🔄
    Formal UNHCR partnership MOU — being finalized for Y2 enhanced sourcing
  • 🔄
    Specific accompaniment protocol — legal, academic, psychosocial in development
  • 🔜
    Y2 target: 15–20 RDP scholars — focus on UNHCR-registered profiles across 5 African regions
Disability Inclusion — Action Plan
10% target · 10-year horizon
  • ✅
    UPHF benchmark — Université Polytechnique Hauts-de-France disability framework reviewed
  • ✅
    Specific needs identified — ad-hoc accommodations put in place for enrolled scholars
  • 🔄
    Campus accessibility diagnostic — both campuses assessed for physical and digital access
  • 🔄
    Disability-specific policy — academic accommodations being formalized
  • 🔄
    Light for the World — specialist NGO partnership in development for sourcing pipeline
  • 🔜
    Local partner mapping — Moroccan disability associations for adapted communication

Oct 2025 – May 2026 · Both campuses

"
Entrepreneurship is no longer theoretical for them. They are building, testing, and preparing to launch. Whether they build their own ventures or lead within existing institutions — one truth holds: the future is entrepreneurial.
— Entrepreneurship Course Report · January–May 2026 · Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program at UM6P
Lessons Learned & Year 2

What We Learned

8 key operational lessons captured to strengthen Year 2 · Cohort 2 targets 84 scholars across 7 academic tracks.

Regulatory
Mobility & Visa Procedures
Regulatory anticipation is critical — start 3 months ahead of intake
Accountability
Document Transmission Delays
Clarity of responsibilities improves operational fluidity for all parties
Logistics
Accommodation & Arrival Experience
Arrival experience is a key quality indicator — must be secured in advance
Equipment
PC Availability at Semester Start
Pedagogical tools must be guaranteed before semester start
Onboarding
Welcome Kit Missing
Standardized kit reinforces belonging and engagement from Day 1
Financial
Allocation Activation Delays
Financial flows must be synchronized with scholar arrival
Academic Planning
Calendar Coordination
Shared consolidated planning is essential to program performance
Governance
Cross-Service Coordination
Transversal governance between schools & services enhances efficiency
Risk Register
Current status
RiskOwnerStatus
20% per African regionOutreach LeadIn Progress
Multi-campus coordinationProgram MgmtActive
SSA communication visibilityComms LeadReinforcing
Disability profile sourcingInclusion LeadIn Progress
Visa & international mobilityProgram MgmtHigh Watch
Cost inflationFinance LeadControlled
Refugee legal access to workDirectorHigh Watch
Year 2 — Cohort 2 Preview
🎯 84 scholars · 7 academic tracks · Mar–Jul 2026 recruitment
🌍 Outreach: Côte d'Ivoire, Mauritanie, Bénin + webinars in Rwanda, Burkina Faso, Cameroon, Niger, Ghana, Chad
🤖 AI-assisted + human screening via 7-step process
🏆 Final pitch competition September 2026 + seed funding
📋 MEL Dashboard operational launch
🛡 Safeguarding policy formal adoption
💪 Strengths
  • 100% women · transformational leadership positioning
  • $42.7M / 10-year institutional commitment
  • 97% S1 academic success · 0 dropouts
  • UM6P co-investment in housing & staff
⚠ Weaknesses
  • Geographic imbalance (20% per region not yet met)
  • Language barrier: English programme, Francophone sourcing
  • Disability profile sourcing difficulty
  • Multi-campus coordination complexity
🌱 Opportunities
  • UM6P as leading women's STEM hub in Africa
  • 575 alumni multiplier effect
  • Morocco as strategic African platform
  • Pan-African alumni network leverage
🚧 Threats
  • Visa & mobility policy barriers
  • SSA geopolitical instability
  • Cost inflation on budgets
  • Legal work barriers for refugee graduates
